I PRAY THEE. Anna (I pray thee) is a conciliatory expression.

We see a faithful man in Israel felling a beam with a borrowed axe; the head comes off and falls into the water. Then at the word of the prophet Elisha-mark it well-"the iron did swim," and the axe head is recovered. (2 Kgs. 6:1-7.) Solid iron swims in water as though it were a cork because Jehovah and his prophets, by faith, have power over the waters. (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, The Mortal Messiah:…

commanded by the same authority that gave the law. The same Being, who gave the law to materials by which they act, can counteract the law. He did so in the instance when Elisha caused iron to swim. We read, that as one was felling a beam, the axe head fell into the water. The man, was much concerned, because it was a borrowed axe. “And the man of God said, Where fell it? And he showed him the…

AMERICAN NEWS. 779
A pROrnET IN Israel.—Every one of our readers who is at all conversant with the Bible must often have been struck with the peculiar and extraordinary history of Israel under the Prophets. They were then, indeed, a peculiar people. A common standard would not measure them, nor was their history to be understood by ordinary minds; for Israel revolved in a higher sphere than that…
unto: Elisha, Behold. now, the place where we.dwell with. thee-is too strait for us.

Elisha, with a power known only to the prophets, had counseled the king of Israel on how and where and when to defend against the warring Syrians. The king of Syria, of course, wished to rid his armies of this prophetic problem. So, and I quote: "Therefore sent he thither horses, and chariots, and a great host: and they came by night, and compassed the city about. "… [They] compassed the city…

When one is outnumbered in the cause of truth it helps to remember how the Lord protects his servants against the armies of the world – as seen in the following story: Elisha had been giving military advice to the king of Israel to help the Israelites avoid the Syrian army. When the Syrian king heard that Elisha’s divine interventions were giving away their military positions, he sent his entire…
unto: Elisha, Behold. now, the place where we.dwell with. thee-is too strait for us.

We are not alone in our mortal struggles. As the prophet Elisha teaches, unseen hosts watch over us. In his day, Syria was at war against Israel, and the prophet Elisha [page 44] counseled the king of Israel against entrapment. The king of Israel followed that counsel and saved himself again and again. This stirred up the king of Syria, who sent by night "horses, and chariots, and a great host,"…

NEITHER WILL I AGAIN SMITE. Via a flood.

Observe that Joseph received an extra blessing, as it is written: EVEN FROM THE GOD OF THY FATHER, HE SHALL HELP THEE. Jacob gave Joseph an inheritance above and below. The inheritance above was given in these words: “from the God of the father”, the place called “heaven”. He added: “And he shall help thee”, to show that this place would not be exchanged for any other place, and his support would…
19 In thee shall be the help of Jacob, And in thee be found the salvation of Israel.
